text: In this video we will present to you two patients with cancer of four percent of the upper and lower part of the right and left side of the brain respectively, who suffered from hypertonic laparoscopic ovarian infarction. The patient is placed on the side and burned as a fetal position as shown in the video. A trocar is placed, three to four hundred percent of the abdomen, outside the rectal-cellular medium. In the medial line, two trocars are placed, twelve millimeters, one subchondriac and the other inside the anterior anolagonia acanthus. Finally, a trocar of ten millimeters is placed in the middle of the distance of the two previous trocars. At the center of the horizontal surface of the middle-to-bottom pole of the left nephron, after placing the trocars and recognizing the anatomical elements, we begin with the solution of the surfaces of the left helix. Recognizing the circumference of the left nephron, which protrudes back from it. Then the opening of the line of the tolt begins, with the removal of the lower helix towards the middle line. The opening begins from the bottom pole of the left nephron and extends downwards, up to the lagonia aegia. It is then extended to the core of the nerve, ensuring that we push the left adductor muscle towards the middle line, thus recognizing the nerve gland and the anus of the nerve. The necessary precondition for the safe removal of muscles on the anterior surface of the nerve is its full movement, giving special emphasis to the anus and the anterior surface.

The surgeon prepares frames and places them visibly on the side of the cell wall. The Ruhmeltourniquet technique is used for the design phase. Pressure is released using xylitol without heat, and the perinephric fat is separated along the joint. Internal pressure is managed using multiple V-Lock sutures.

The surgeon completes the internal printing using a v-lock suture technique, followed by the thermal structure process lasting approximately fourteen minutes. An external nephrology procedure is performed using a number one vikril suture and hemolog. The nephron is fully mobilized and placed inside a collection bag. The bronchitis is placed after the left part of the nerve, and the trachea is removed under vision to control possible hemorrhage. The final external image shows the closure of the surgical injury.
